what_it_does: This file is a ledger entry documenting an auxiliary machine-learning model (sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2) that is present on disk but not treated as a primary endpoint; it records metadata, likely origin, intended use, open questions, and a link back to the models hub.
observations: Frontmatter declares type=model, status=auxiliary, runtime=not-yet-served, date_added=present-before-substrate-setup
Model is sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 6-layer MiniLM, 384 embedding dimensions, 888 MB on disk
Likely downloaded automatically by the sentence-transformers library as a default embedder
Tagged as foundation, models, embedding, auxiliary, small
Listed as a tooling default rather than a deliberate 'Tim-need'
Open questions ask whether to keep the 888 MB on disk and which tools auto-load it
Connects only to the models index hub
standouts: Explicitly marked auxiliary / not a primary endpoint
888 MB model present on disk with runtime status 'not-yet-served'
Origin inferred from prior sentence-transformers auto-load rather than a deliberate pull
Open cleanup/retention decision is the main action item
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, filename, status, runtime, date_added, and tags
Includes an 'At a glance' table summarizing key metadata
Neutral, inventory-style tone without ranking or advocacy
Sections follow a standard pattern: Source, Fits these needs, Open at this entry, Connects to
concerns: Runtime says 'not-yet-served' but the model is present on disk, creating a status/availability mismatch
Provenance is speculative ('likely downloaded by sentence-transformers')
No explicit list of which tools or services currently load it
Retention decision is left open with no owner or criteria
888 MB is described as 'small' in tags but still flagged as non-trivial
notes: A neutral inventory stub for an accidentally-cached auxiliary embedding model, mainly asking whether to keep it and which tooling pins it.
questions: Which specific tools or services currently auto-load this model?
Is it referenced by any running pipeline, test, or service?
What is the exact on-disk path and cache location?
When and by which process was it actually downloaded?
What criteria will decide retention or removal?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to document an auxiliary model's presence; the file does exactly that, adding metadata and open questions without claiming primary status.
apparent_themes: Inventory hygiene and accidental asset tracking
Provenance inference for pre-existing cached artifacts
Deliberate model slate versus tooling defaults
intention_signals: The entry seems to be trying to surface an incidental, non-deliberate model asset so the team can decide whether to pin tooling embedders, remove the cached weights, or formally keep it as an auxiliary resource.
novelty: It elevates a library-default cached model to a first-class ledger entry and explicitly labels it as outside the deliberate embedding slate, rather than leaving it invisible in the cache.
contribution: It contributes a factual record of an on-disk auxiliary model's identity, size, architecture, inferred origin, and unresolved retention questions, giving a fused system a clean starting point for cleanup or ownership decisions.
summary_for_embedding: A ledger entry for the 384-dimension sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 auxiliary embedding model, 888 MB, present on disk before deliberate model pulls, used as a tooling default rather than a primary endpoint.
intention_for_embedding: To document an incidentally cached auxiliary model and flag open decisions about retention and which tools auto-load it.
